macro_segments.title: Aggadic Discourse on Intent and Righteousness: Tamar, Zimri, and the Power of Motivation

micro_segments.title: Opening drasha on the praised king who acknowledges sin and brings a korban
Extension to the righteous and wicked: same act can be mitzvah or aveirah depending on intent
Tamar's fornication as mitzvah versus Zimri's as aveirah: the role of leshem shamayim
Rav Nachman's radical statement: aveirah lishmah greater than mitzvah shelo lishmah
Proof from Yael and Sisera: her act superior to Matriarchs as it lacked personal pleasure
Gemara's qualification: Rav Yehuda's principle that mitzvah shelo lishmah still has value
Examination of potential contradiction: how aveirah lishmah can exceed mitzvah shelo lishmah
Lecturer's analysis: antinomian dangers of this teaching and legitimate exceptions
Discussion of Esther and Achashverosh case and Mossad agents example
Rav Kook's perspective on passion versus apathy; importance of not being indifferent

macro_segments.title: Seven Intercourses of Sisera and the Distinction Between Intent and Action

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan on seven intercourses of Sisera derived from repeated language in verse
Question: how can Yael's act be lishmah when she experienced pleasure
Rabbi Yochanan quoting Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai: good of the wicked is evil to the righteous
Repetition of principle: even mitzvah shelo lishmah has value; shelo lishmah leads to lishmah
Merit of Balak the wicked through 42 korbanot: Ruth descended and brought David
God does not withhold reward even for pleasant conversation
Lot's daughters' names and divine restrictions on Moab and Ammon
Priority of mitzvah: older daughter merits four generations before Ammonite line

macro_segments.title: Halachic Rules for Korban Chatat: Common Person, Kohen Gadol, and Nasi Distinction

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: from people of the land exclude Kohen Gadol and Nasi from standard chatat
Why exclude Kohen Gadol: special conditions (psak and ma'aseh) versus just ma'aseh
Why exclude Nasi: clarification through Rav Zevid's case of eating chelev as commoner then becoming king
Rav Zevid on Rava: case of half-eaten kezayis as commoner, completed as king within eating time
Question: whether status change mid-act combines the act or breaks it
Rava's query to Rav Nachman: does kingship interrupt sin; case of appointed king then removed
Unresolved: does his status as king in middle of act affect combining; comparison to דחוי dichuy
Rav Zeira's question on safek chelev: if status changes during uncertainty, do we bring asham taluy
According to Rabbanan: status of sin determines korban, not status of awareness
According to Rabbi Shimon: both sin and awareness must have same status to bring korban
Teiku: unresolved whether asham taluy applies when underlying chatat status changes

macro_segments.title: Definition and Status of Mumar: Categories of Apostasy and Exclusion from Korbanos

micro_segments.title: Baraita excluding mumar from bringing korban; Rabbi Shimon bar Yosi's source from 'asher lo se'asenah'
Rabbi Shimon's drasha: shav b'yedi'ah—would not have sinned if he knew better
Lo shav b'yedi'ah: would have sinned anyway; two approaches—personal status versus action
Practical difference: mumar le'chelev eating dam; does global mumar status affect other prohibitions
Rava's position: mumar in one area does not affect status in another unrelated area
Case of mumar le'teivon eating neveilah mistaken for shuman: two definitions of mumar
Baraita distinguishing: one opinion says mumar l'teivon; other says eizehu mumar—definition disagreement
Rabbi Yehuda's addition: even wearer of kilayim is mumar; clarification of terms
Distinction: mumar le'teivon (appetite-driven) versus mumar le'hachis (principled rejection)
Dispute: mumar only for d'oraita versus mumar even for famous d'rabbanan prohibitions
Rav Acha and Ravina: le'teivon is mumar versus le'hachis also mumar; definition of min
Min must reject entire system (avodat kochavim), not just one prohibition
Question from case of eating one flea: still called mumar, not min; supports le'hachis is mumar view
Gemara: one-time act to taste forbidden thing not necessarily le'hachis; could be appetite-driven

macro_segments.title: Definition of Nasi (King) and Mashiach (Anointed Kohen Gadol): Status Requirements and Distinctions

micro_segments.title: Baraita: who is nasi—not head of tribe but only king from perspective of mitzva authority
Question to Rabbi Yochanan: whether Rabbi himself would have status of nasi for se'ir korban
Answer: You are not sole authority; Reish Galuта of Bavel competes for authority
Objection: kings of Israel and Beit David each brought se'ir; doesn't require sole authority
Response: There they are not subject to each other; here you are subject to Bavel authority
Rav Safra's version: distinction between shevet (staff/physical power) and mechokek (lawgiver)
Baraita on Davidic line: Reish Galuta has staff; Rabbi and descendants of Hillel are lawgivers
Question whether ירושה yerushah (inheritance) affects these laws; response about Bavel's role

macro_segments.title: Definition of Nasi (King) and Mashiach (Anointed Kohen Gadol): Status Requirements and Distinctions (continued)

micro_segments.title: Mashiach defined: the anointed Kohen Gadol; contrast to Kohen with only vestments
Mishnah from Megilla: no difference in most halachot between anointed and vestment-only Kohen Gadol
Exception: par brought only by anointed Kohen Gadol, not by kevush begadim version
Kohen Gadol must marry virgin; cannot become tamei for relatives; no rending clothes
Murderer released from ir miklat if Kohen Gadol dies; applies even if other Kohen Gadol alive

macro_segments.title: The Anointing Oil: Miraculous Properties, Usage, and Designation of Kohanim and Kings

micro_segments.title: Baraita: shemen hamishcha that Moshe made; dispute on boiling versus soaking method
Rabbi Yehuda: boil oil with roots; Rabbi Yosi: roots first soaked in water to prevent absorption
Rabbi Yehuda's response: multiple miracles occurred with oil, not just one
Oil miracle: started as 12 log, anointed Mishkan, vessels, Aaron, sons for seven days
Oil remains and will be available for future; gematria of 'zeh' equals 12
Kohen Gadol son of Kohen Gadol requires anointing; drasha from 'ha-kohen ha-mashiach tachtav'
Kings generally not anointed if succession clear; exceptions for disputed kingship (Solomon, Yehoash)
Distinction: Kohen Gadol not automatic inheritance (needs worthiness); kingship presumed yerushah

topical_tags.term: leshem shamayim (for the sake of Heaven)
mitzvah shelo lishmah (commandment without proper intent)
tzaddikim yelchu bam uposhim yichashlu bam (righteous walk in them, wicked stumble)
